title: ranunculus cassubicus subsp. pawlowskii
intro: Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. pawlowskii is a subspecies of the greater celandine plant, known for its bright yellow flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus cassubicus subsp. pawlowskii include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting liver and skin health. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as skin lesions and digestive issues.
